top_signals.title: How has India dealt with the economic hit from the Iran war?
top_signals.summary: Also in this newsletter: can India’s virtual cockroaches survive in the real world?
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Decisions taken by the Governing Council of the ECB (in addition to decisions setting interest rates)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Biocon's Mazumdar Shaw Says Firm On Track To Be Top Insulin Maker
top_signals.summary: Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar Shaw says she is confident that the company's investment and capacity expansion in Insulin will make the company the top hub for the drug in the next five years. She speaks in an exclusive conversation with Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Goldman-backed Go soars 21% after biggest Japan IPO this year
top_signals.summary: The successful debut of the taxi-hailing app provider offers a much-needed tailwind for Japan's IPO market.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Salesforce to buy AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6 billion to boost agentic offerings
top_signals.summary: Businesses are accelerating their agentic offerings for enterprises as competition heats up.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Finland brings charges against cargo ship officers for cutting submarine cables
top_signals.summary: According to the deputy prosecutor general, the ship’s officers have now been charged with “having damaged two subsea telecommunications cables and of having attempted to damage a total of eight other subsea connections.”
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, April 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit decreased in April 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit decreased from $56.6 billion in March (revised) to $55.9 billion in April, as exports increased more than imports. The goods deficit decreased $2.4 billion in April to $83.7 billion. The services surplus decreased $1.7 billion in April to $27.8 billion. Full Text
